rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee declarations in component type scopes for interface behavior; use implementation scopes for binding assignments.
Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks to contain all AGREE contract elements.
generic_rules: Refer to component ports only through valid variables or outputs in type-level contracts.
Define constants and equations in component type annexes when describing interface behavior.
Encode boolean logic using standard AGREE expression syntax with clear variable references.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `assume "label": expression;` and `guarantee "label": expression;` for behavioral constraints.
Use `eq name : type = expression;` for derived values and `const name : type = value;` for fixed values.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL property names like Compute_Execution_Time directly in AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants.
Avoid placing implementation-specific assign clauses in component type annexes.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and their automatic scoping in AGREE
Model fusion and architecture redesign during repair
